student_name: Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari
hub_subtitle: Inglês do dia a dia — apresentar-se, rotina e conversação

lesson.menu_desc: Talking about your life experiences so far -- the things you have and have never done, with the present perfect and ever / never -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 12 -- Experiences So Far
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 12 -- Experiences So Far
lesson.grammar_point: present perfect with ever and never for life experiences

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: in or to a foreign country
a chance to do something good
to travel around a place to discover it
the best or most memorable part of an experience
to succeed in doing something difficult
the whole period of a person's life
to offer to help without being paid
very exciting and a bit scary
to try to do something difficult
ready to face danger or difficulty
a list of things you want to do in your life

paras: People sometimes ask me if I have had an interesting life, and I always smile, because I have. I am the kind of person who says yes to almost every opportunity. I have traveled abroad several times, and I have explored cities I once only saw in films. I have never been the type to sit still; for me, every trip is a chance to learn something new.

Not all of my favorite experiences have been far from home. A few years ago, I decided to volunteer at a community clinic, and it quickly became one of the most rewarding things I have ever done. I have also attempted things that scared me. I have gone diving in the ocean, and once I even tried rock climbing. I am not always brave, but I have learned that the thrilling moments are the ones I remember best.

Of course, there are still many things I have never done. I have never seen snow, and traveling to a cold country is high on my bucket list. I would love to accomplish that dream one day, maybe with my family. When I look back at my life so far, I feel grateful. The highlight is not one single moment, but the habit of always saying: why not? It really is the trip of a lifetime.
reading.source: Alumni by Better -- B1 reading, a learner shares her life experiences so far

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: It gives strict rules you must follow before you travel abroad.
Ana Paula shares her life experiences so far and her open attitude of always saying yes.
It explains why trying new things is dangerous and should be avoided.

Ana Paula has already seen snow many times.
The text says: 'I have never seen snow, and traveling to a cold country is high on my bucket list.'

The text says: 'the thrilling moments are the ones I remember best.'

gapfill.text: The present perfect for experiences. Use have or has + the past participle to talk about experiences in your life when the exact time is not important (I have traveled abroad). Add 'ever' in questions (Have you ever tried diving?) and 'never' for no experience (I have never seen snow). We do not say the exact date; we care about the experience, not when it happened.

bank.label: Useful sentences to talk about your experiences
items: I have traveled abroad several times.
I have never been to Asia.
Volunteering is the most rewarding thing I have ever done.
Have you ever tried something thrilling?
Seeing snow is on my bucket list.
It was the trip of a lifetime.
I have accomplished one of my biggest dreams.
What is the bravest thing you have ever done?

lf.text: Notice the pattern: have or has + the past participle for life experiences. Add 'ever' in questions (Have you ever...?) and 'never' for no experience (I have never...).

items.situation: Have you ever traveled abroad? Tell me about it.
tips: No, I have never traveled abroad, but ...

items.situation: What is the most thrilling thing you have ever done?
tips: The most thrilling thing I have ever done is ...
I have never done anything very thrilling, but ...

items.situation: Name one thing on your bucket list and say why.
tips: ... is on my bucket list because ...

items.situation: Have you ever volunteered or helped in your community?
tips: No, I have never volunteered, but I would like to ...

items.situation: What is one thing you have never tried but really want to?
tips: I have never tried ..., but I want to.

items: Tell me three things you have done in your life. Start each one with 'I have ...'.
Now tell me two things you have never done but want to try. Use 'I have never ...'.
Talk about your life experiences so far with no notes. Mix things you have done and things you have never done.
A friend asks: 'What is the bravest thing you have ever done?' Answer with a full short story.

answerkey.title: Reveal the model answer
list: Model: 'I have traveled abroad several times, and I have explored some amazing cities. One of the most rewarding things I have ever done was to volunteer at a clinic. I have attempted a few brave things, like diving, but I have never seen snow. Traveling to a cold country is high on my bucket list, and I would love to accomplish that dream one day.'
Experiences you have: have or has + past participle (I have traveled, she has volunteered).
No experience: I have never + past participle (I have never seen snow).
Questions: Have you ever + past participle? (Have you ever tried diving?)
answerkey.note: Use the present perfect for life experiences when the exact time is not important. Add 'ever' in questions and 'never' for no experience.

listenings.text: People often ask me about the most exciting things I have done in my life, and honestly, I have been quite lucky. I have traveled abroad many times, and I have explored cities on almost every continent. The highlight of my life was probably climbing a mountain in Nepal; it was thrilling and a little scary, but I felt so brave at the top. I have also volunteered at an animal shelter, which was one of the most rewarding things I have ever done. There is still one big thing on my bucket list: I have never seen the northern lights, and I really want to. I believe every new experience, big or small, is an opportunity to grow. Life is short, so I try to say yes to adventure whenever I can.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I was just thinking about all the things I want to do in my life, and I had to call you. So far, I have accomplished a few of my dreams: I have lived abroad, and I have learned to scuba dive. But there is so much I have never tried! I have never been to Asia, and it has been my dream for years. Next summer, I am finally going to attempt something a little crazy: I want to go skydiving. I know it sounds scary, but I think it will be the most thrilling experience of my lifetime. Helping others is important to me too, so I have started to volunteer at a local library. Anyway, tell me: what is the bravest thing you have ever done? Call me back, I really want to know. Bye!

extra_audio.text: I have always loved trying new things in my life. A few years ago, I finally traveled abroad for the first time. There, I explored an old city, and it became the highlight of my trip. Later, I attempted something brave and went diving in the sea. Now, seeing the northern lights is the only thing left on my bucket list.
